do not share this page

THEY ALREADY KNOW YOU'RE HERE

my name is hank handshaw

i'm not saying how old years old. i live in california. i used to live in redacted before that and redacted before that. i move a lot. i have to.

i'm writing this because i don't know how much longer i'll be able to

i'm not a journalist. i'm not a whistleblower. i'm not anything. i'm just a guy who started noticing things and couldn't stop. i didn't ask for this. i didn't go looking for it. it found me the same way it finds everyone except most people look away and i didn't

that was my mistake

it started in i can't say when exactly. i was living in redacted working at redacted doing doesn't matter. normal life. normal job. normal everything.

then i noticed a car outside my apartment. white. redacted make and model. parked in the same spot three days in a row. no one ever got in or out. the windows were tinted past legal. on the fourth day it was gone and there was a different car. gray. same spot. same tint.

most people would say that's nothing

most people would be wrong

i started keeping track. writing things down. dates times locations plate numbers when i could get them. i kept it in a notebook at first then i realized a notebook can be taken so i moved it to a hard drive then i realized a hard drive can be accessed remotely so i moved it to paper again but different paper in a different location that i change every not saying how often

the more i documented the more there was to document. like they weren't even trying to hide it anymore. like my awareness was part of the plan. like they wanted me to see it because seeing it and not being believed is worse than not seeing it at all

that's the real punishment. knowing.

i lost my job at redacted because i started redacted. they said it was redacted but i know the real reason. i was asking questions about redacted and someone made a call and that was it. you don't get fired on a day of the week for something that happened on a different day unless the timeline was decided before the reason was

i lost my apartment after that. couldn't pay rent. moved to redacted. then redacted. then the cabin. the cabin is the only place that feels clean.

i don't talk to my family member anymore. or my family member. they think i'm their word not mine. they don't understand that the reason i sound the way i sound is because i've seen what i've seen and you can't unsee it. you can't go back to normal after you've watched a man redacted in an alley and nobody calls it in. you can't just eat breakfast after that.

i don't know who they are. not exactly. i have theories but i'm not going to write them here because naming them is how they justify escalating. as long as i'm just a guy with a page they can monitor me. the second i name names i become a threat and threats get dealt with

all i know is this:

they are organized. they have access to infrastructure. they operate in patterns. they use fire. they don't care about collateral. and they have been watching me for length of time and i can prove it

what follows is my evidence

photos i took myself. timestamps intact. locations documented. i haven't altered anything. i don't need to. the truth doesn't need editing.

if you're reading this and you work for them you already know everything on this page

if you're reading this and you don't work for them then pay attention

because once you see it you'll see it everywhere

and then you'll understand why i am the way i am

. . . . .
04.08.26 — 11:47 PM

something happened tonight

i was driving through the canyon. banham canyon. same route same time i always take it because i need to know if they'll do something and tonight they did

road was empty. completely empty. i checked i always check. there was nothing ahead of me for miles just dark asphalt and guardrail

then one second later a car was there

one. second.

coming straight at me. head on. on a road with no turnoffs no driveways no intersections for 4 miles in either direction

the car wasn't there and then it was

they wanted it to look like an accident a drunk driver on a canyon road at midnight nobody would question it nobody would look twice that's how they operate everything has to look normal

i survived. barely.

the car didn't stop. didn't swerve. didn't slow down. just kept going like a job completed like checking something off a list.

they tried to kill me tonight and no one will believe me

i need to stop shaking before i keep writing

. . . . .
04.05.26 — 3:12 AM

drove past the scrapyard outside sandy shores the one with the sign that says SCRAP METAL BOUGHT

there's a commercial airliner sitting in there

a whole plane

fuselage intact FlyUS livery still visible and right next to it a cell tower

no crash reports in the news no FAA no investigation no nothing just a plane sitting in a desert scrapyard like someone parked it there and everybody agreed to not talk about it

SANDY SHORES SCRAPYARD — FLYUS FUSELAGE — CELL TOWER ADJACENT

always a cell tower

always always always a cell tower

they need line of sight. that's how the signal works. that's how they track the frequency. i don't know what frequency yet but i will

i will

. . . . .
04.03.26 — 7:14 PM

came up to the cabin

the one nobody knows about coordinates removed by me not them by me

it's the only place where the air feels different where my head doesn't buzz where i can think without wondering if the thinking is mine or if they're putting thoughts in the signal

no cell towers in line of sight. trees block satellite view. no service.

took a photo of myself out front because if i disappear at least there's a record. timestamp proof. gps in the metadata if anyone ever looks which they won't because nobody looks

the sky was pink when i got here

i don't trust peace anymore. peace is what it looks like right before they do something.

i think they found the cabin. the truck was moved when i came back from the ridge. two inches to the left. i measure every time. every single time. two inches doesn't happen by itself. gravity doesn't shift a parked vehicle on flat ground. someone was here. someone sat in my truck and adjusted the seat and put it back but not exactly right. they never get it exactly right. that's how i know. that's always how i know. the seat was warm. or maybe i imagined the warm part. i don't know anymore. i don't know what's real and what's the pattern making me see things that aren't there. but the two inches. the two inches is real. i measured.

03.29.26 — 1:33 PM

found a car burning in the desert

middle of nowhere. no other vehicles. no driver. no tracks leading away. just a car engulfed. hood blown fifteen feet from the chassis

that's not a car fire. civilian engines don't do that. that's a thermite charge under the block. military grade. the kind that doesn't leave traces the kind that turns a VIN number into vapor

DESERT — VEHICLE DESTRUCTION — NO WITNESSES

you don't stay near their cleanup sites

you take the photo and you leave and you don't look back because looking back is how they see your face

03.27.26 — 6:08 PM

police cruiser on fire

wedged into a wall on not writing the street. burning. no officers. no fire trucks. no ambulance. no tape. nothing.

a police car. burning. in a city. and nobody came.

ABANDONED — DIAL 911 ON THE DOOR

real cops don't abandon cruisers. real fires get responded to. unless someone makes sure they don't. unless the dispatch system is theirs too.

how deep does this go

all the way down probably

yeah

all the way down

03.25.26 — 9:44 PM

three convertibles. same model. same intersection. white orange gray. top down on all three. drivers staring straight ahead not at each other not at traffic not at anything just forward like mannequins propped behind steering wheels

and then some guy just ran through the middle of them

sprinting. full speed. between the cars like he was trying to get away from something. leather jacket. didn't look back. the drivers didn't react. none of them flinched. none of them turned their heads. three people in convertibles with the tops down and a man runs through their cars at full sprint and nobody reacts

that's not normal. that's not how people work. you see someone running through traffic you look. you turn. you do something. unless you already know he's coming. unless the running is part of it too.

THREE — IDENTICAL — FORMATION — RUNNER BETWEEN VEHICLES

who was he running from

or who was he running to

was he a distraction so i'd look at him instead of them

did it work

yeah it worked

i looked at him

and by the time i looked back the light changed and the three cars moved in sync same speed same spacing like nothing happened

three is their number. three cars. three days between incidents. three bars of signal before it drops. three flickering lights before the honk.

three three three

i'm doing it again aren't i

seeing the pattern

or is the pattern seeing me

03.22.26 — 11:15 PM

flipped car on the shoulder. red. upside down. left there like garbage

but the damage is wrong. the roof caved from the inside. cars don't flip and cave inward they compress downward that's physics that's how gravity works something was removed from this car before it was staged

DAMAGE PATTERN INCONSISTENT

and there was someone on the ground next to it

not moving

i waited twenty minutes for an ambulance

nobody came

in a city of millions nobody came

20+ MINUTES — NO RESPONSE

i left before they could place me at the scene. that's what they want. put me near their work so they can pin it on me later. frame the paranoid guy. who's gonna believe him

right

who's gonna believe him

no accelerant smell. chemical.

i think he talked.

this is what happens when you talk

this is why i type instead of speak

this is why i don't call anyone

this is why the cabin

03.15.26 — 2:20 PM

went to the supermarket on i'm not saying which one

the clerk is dead

behind the counter. slumped over. blood on the wall behind him on the shelves on everything

the register was still open. the store was still open. the door was unlocked. the lights were on. the candy was still on the counter and the chips were still in the rack and the batteries were still hanging on the pegboard and there was a dead man behind the counter and the store was still open

i walked in to buy water

just water

nobody else was in the store. no customers. no second employee. no alarm. the security camera in the corner had a red light on but i don't think it was recording because i don't think it's a real camera. i think it's for show. i think they all are.

the door chimed when i walked in. a normal chime. like everything was normal. like there wasn't a body three feet from the register.

who leaves a store open with a dead clerk

who does that

someone who wants it to be found

someone who wants a specific person to find it

i didn't buy the water

. . . . .
03.12.26 — 4:45 PM

body in the desert

face down against a rock. green shirt. blue hat. tattoo on his arm. not moving. not breathing.

just lying there like he laid down and stopped

SANDY SHORES — NO ID — NO TRACKS

there were no footprints leading to him. i checked. the sand was smooth. untouched. like he appeared there the same way the car appeared on the canyon road

people don't end up face down in the desert with no tracks. you walk somewhere or you get carried somewhere. either way there are marks. there are always marks.

unless he was dropped

from what

i don't want to think about from what

. . . . .
03.09.26 — 11:30 AM

motorcyclist down on the street. helmet still on. bike on its side. pool spreading under his head.

right next to a utility box

a utility box

DOWNTOWN — NEXT TO UTILITY BOX — SIGNAL INFRASTRUCTURE

every time. every single time. there's infrastructure nearby. cell tower. utility box. junction cabinet. transformer. they need hardware close to where things happen because the hardware is how they make things happen

this wasn't an accident. nobody crashes a motorcycle in a straight line on dry road in daylight. the road was flat. no debris. no oil. no reason to go down.

unless the bike was told to go down

unless the signal from the box made the electronics cut out for half a second

half a second is all it takes at 40 mph

they can do that right

yeah they can do that

i know they can do that

. . . . .
03.06.26 — 8:15 PM

raining. following a white car because it had been behind me for eleven blocks and then suddenly it was in front of me and i don't remember it passing me

got close enough to see the back

there's a device on the trunk

UNKNOWN DEVICE — REAR TRUNK — RAIN — 8:15 PM

small. green and red. strapped or magnetized to the body panel near the tail light. could be a tracker. could be a transmitter. could be

wait

no

no no no no no

that's not a tracker

that's not a tracker

that's a bomb

that's a sticky bomb on the back of that car and i was three feet from it i was RIGHT THERE i was close enough to read the wiring and if it had gone off i would have

i would have

ok

ok ok ok

i turned off at the next block. lights off. drove dark for a mile. parked in an alley and sat there with my hands on the wheel shaking for i don't know how long

twenty minutes maybe. maybe more. the rain was loud on the roof. i couldn't tell if the ringing in my ears was real or if it was the signal or if it was just my blood

was it meant for him or was it meant for me

was i supposed to be next to it when it went off

was the eleven blocks of following me just to get me close enough

the rain helps. the rain scrambles some of the signals.

i think

i hope

i need to check under my car now

i need to check under my car every time now

. . . . .
plane. no crash report. cell tower. WHY.
burning cop car. no dispatch.
three. runner. nobody flinched.
thermite. desert. no VIN left.
he talked. look what happened.
store still open. clerk dead. door chimed.
utility box. always infrastructure.
not a transmitter. a bomb. i was 3 feet away.
last photo. if i'm gone start here.

i keep a list in my head

things that don't add up things that shouldn't happen things that only happen around me

vehicles on empty roads. always head-on. always at night.

cell towers at every site. every single one.

no emergency response. ever. 20 minute gaps. in a city.

everything burns within 48 hours. fire. always fire.

three three three three three

the list keeps getting longer

it never gets shorter

things don't stop being true just because nobody else sees them

if you're reading this you're already part of it

not because you chose to be

because they let you find this page

nothing on the internet is accidental every link is a path they've already mapped

i'm not crazy

i'm the only one paying attention

or maybe that's what crazy people say

i don't know anymore

i don't know

ok i hear it again

the humming

it's back

i'm going to close the laptop now

if this page stops updating assume the worst
